examples: The first clouds are thin, wispy cirrus, followed by sheets of cirrus and cirrostratus and altostratus.
When cirrostratus clouds are disturbed by planes or strong winds, the droplets can freeze instantly or evaporate, the latter of which will form the hole.
These clouds occur within cirrostratus clouds, which are often composed of ice crystals and drops of water that are below freezing temperature but still in liquid form.
Cirrostratus is a "very subtle" cloud that can be nothing more than a milky whitening of the sky.
